metadata.title: Test Logger Rollout - Add Logging to All Test Files

implementation_phase.objective: Add structured logging via TestLogger to all 62 test files that currently lack logging. This enables comprehensive failure context capture and AI-assisted troubleshooting across the entire test suite.

logging_pattern.description: Standard pattern to apply to all test files. Keep it minimal but meaningful.

what_to_log.log_this: Major test steps (navigation, form submission, assertions)
User context (who is logged in, what role)

rollout_batches.description: Files organized by directory/category for systematic rollout. Each batch can be done independently.

batch.name: Marketplace & Search
batch.logging_focus: Search queries, filter selections, case IDs viewed

batch.name: Negotiations & Proposals
batch.logging_focus: Proposal IDs, negotiation states, fee amounts, user roles

batch.name: My Tasks & Dashboard
batch.logging_focus: Task counts, filter states, tab switches

batch.name: Case Management
batch.logging_focus: Case IDs, status transitions, document uploads

batch.name: Referral Network
batch.logging_focus: Attorney IDs, firm IDs, connection actions

minimal_logging_example.description: For simpler tests, minimal logging is acceptable. Focus on test start/end and any important data context.

automation_script.description: Optional: Script to add basic logging boilerplate to files. Review output manually before committing.
automation_script.script:
#!/bin/bash # add-logger-import.sh # Adds logger import to test files that don't have it
cd attorney_share_mvp_web/automation/playwright/tests
for file in $(find . -name "*.spec.ts" -type f); do # Check if already has logger import if ! grep -q "import.*getLogger" "$file"; then echo "Adding logger import to: $file"
# Add import after first import statement sed -i '' "1a\\ import { getLogger } from '../../utils/test-logger' " "$file" fi done
echo "Done. Review changes with: git diff"

verification.verify_import: # Count files with logger import grep -r "import.*getLogger" tests/ | wc -l # Should be 67 (all test files)
verification.verify_usage: # Count files using logger.info grep -r "logger.info" tests/ | wc -l # Should have multiple occurrences per file
verification.run_tests:
# Run a batch and verify logging works FRONTEND_URL=https://app.attorneyshare.info \ BACKEND_URL=https://api.attorneyshare.info \ npx playwright test tests/signin/ --reporter=list
# Check for logger output in console

success_criteria.criterion: All test files have at least test start/end logging
Important data context (IDs, states) is logged
All tests still pass after adding logging

notes.note: This is the most time-consuming phase but adds the most value. Consider doing high-priority batches (1-3) first for immediate impact.
Logging should enhance debugging, not clutter output. When in doubt, log less.
Page Objects already have logging shortcuts via BasePage. Focus on test-level context (what test is doing, why).
